steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which question the two authors would most likely disagree about answering. A correct disagreement question must produce opposite yes/no answers from each text: Text 1 says one thing; Text 2 says the opposite.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Do other art forms than poetry require substantial investments of time and money?: Both texts implicitly agree that other art forms demand more resources. No real disagreement. ✗
  • Choice B — Should poets try to gain experience working in nonartistic fields as a way of improving their poetry?: Neither text addresses this prescription. No basis for disagreement. ✗
  • Choice C — Does professional training in poetry affect the quality of poets' work?: Text 1 does not address training or quality; Text 2 mentions professional training only as a barrier to publication, not a quality judgment. No clear opposition. ✗
  • Choice D — Is working a full-time job a major impediment to practicing the art of poetry?: Text 1 answers no — Lorde argues poets can write "between shifts, in the hospital pantry, on the subway," so full-time work is not a block. Text 2 answers yes — extensive practice "requires time not often available to those who must also work full-time jobs." Direct opposition. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
